Title: Register patient with the OpenMRS ID identifier type generated by the IDGEN auto-generation source
Preconditions: Logged in registration clerk; IdentifierSourcebased OpenMRS ID auto-generation configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Register a patient | 2. Complete required demographics | 3. Select identifier type OpenMRS ID | 4. Choose auto-generate | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Patient is created with a system-generated OpenMRS ID matching the configured Luhn mod-30 format and appears on the dashboard header

Title: Register patient with a manually entered Old Identification Number identifier type
Preconditions: Logged in clerk; Old Identification Number identifier type enabled and not auto-generated
Test_Steps: 1. Open Register a patient | 2. Enter demographics | 3. Add identifier type Old Identification Number | 4. Manually type the value | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Patient saved; the manually entered identifier is stored verbatim and displayed in the patient identifiers list

Title: Validate a Luhn mod-30 check digit accepts a correctly formed OpenMRS ID
Preconditions: LuhnMod30IdentifierValidator assigned to the identifier type
Test_Steps: 1. Open Register a patient | 2. Select identifier type using Luhn mod-30 validator | 3. Enter an identifier whose check digit is correct | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Identifier passes validation and the patient is saved without error

Title: Reject an OpenMRS ID with an incorrect Luhn mod-30 check digit
Preconditions: LuhnMod30IdentifierValidator assigned to the identifier type
Test_Steps: 1. Open Register a patient | 2. Select Luhn mod-30 identifier type | 3. Enter an identifier with a wrong final check character | 4. Attempt to save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with an inline validation error indicating an invalid check digit; patient is not created

Title: Reject an identifier containing characters outside the Luhn mod-30 allowed base-30 alphabet
Preconditions: LuhnMod30IdentifierValidator assigned; base-30 alphabet excludes I,O,Q
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Select Luhn mod-30 identifier type | 3. Enter an identifier containing excluded letters | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation fails with a message that the identifier contains invalid characters for the format

Title: Decision table for Verhoeff vs Luhn vs no-validator identifier types
Preconditions: Three identifier types configured: Verhoeff validator, Luhn mod-30 validator, no validator
Test_Steps: 1. For each identifier type, enter a value valid under that scheme and a value invalid under it | 2. Save each combination | 3. Record acceptance
Test_Data: Rules: Verhoeff+valid=accept; Verhoeff+invalid=reject; Luhn+valid=accept; Luhn+invalid=reject; NoValidator+any=accept
Expected_Result: System accepts and rejects exactly per the decision table; no false accept on an invalid check digit

Title: Boundary on minimum identifier length for a fixed-format validated type
Preconditions: Identifier type with a regex/format validator enforcing a minimum length
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Enter an identifier one character below minimum | 3. Save | 4. Re-enter at exact minimum | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Below-minimum value is rejected; the exact-minimum value is accepted

Title: Boundary on maximum identifier length for a format-constrained type
Preconditions: Identifier type with a maximum length constraint
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Enter an identifier at the exact maximum length | 3. Save | 4. Enter one character above maximum | 5. Save
Expected_Result: At-maximum value is accepted; over-maximum value is rejected with a length error

Title: Prevent creating a second patient with an identifier value already in use for the same identifier type
Preconditions: An existing patient already owns identifier value DUP-100 of type Old Identification Number
Test_Steps: 1. Register a new patient | 2. Add identifier type Old Identification Number | 3. Enter the already-used value | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked; system reports the identifier is already in use and the duplicate patient is not created

Title: Allow the same identifier string under two different identifier types when uniqueness is scoped per type
Preconditions: Two identifier types both allow value 555; uniqueness enforced per identifier type
Test_Steps: 1. Patient A gets value 555 of type X | 2. Register patient B | 3. Assign value 555 of type Y | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Patient B is saved; the same string is permitted because uniqueness is enforced within each type, not across types

Title: Enforce case- and whitespace-normalized uniqueness so trailing spaces do not bypass the duplicate check
Preconditions: Existing patient owns identifier ABC123
Test_Steps: 1. Register a new patient | 2. Enter the same identifier with leading/trailing spaces and altered case | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System normalizes and blocks the duplicate; whitespace/case variation does not create a second record

Title: Concurrent duplicate prevention when two clerks save the same new identifier simultaneously
Preconditions: Two sessions open Register a patient with the same intended manual identifier
Test_Steps: 1. Session 1 enters identifier and clicks Save | 2. Session 2 enters identical identifier and clicks Save within the same window | 3. Observe results
Expected_Result: Exactly one save succeeds; the other is rejected by a unique constraint, preventing a duplicate-identifier collision

Title: Database unique constraint prevents duplicate active identifiers at the persistence layer
Preconditions: DB access to patient_identifier table; voided flag semantics understood
Test_Steps: 1. Insert a patient_identifier row with value UQ-1 | 2. Insert a second non-voided row with identifier UQ-1 of the same type | 3. Observe constraint behavior
Expected_Result: The second insert is rejected by the unique index on (identifier, identifier_type) for non-voided rows

Title: Voided identifier value can be reassigned to a different patient
Preconditions: Patient A had identifier REUSE-1 which was voided/removed
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm REUSE-1 is voided on patient A | 2. Register patient B | 3. Assign REUSE-1 | 4. Save
Test_Data: Value=REUSE-1; voided on A; assigned to B
Expected_Result: Patient B saves successfully because the prior identifier was voided and no active duplicate exists

Title: Add multiple identifiers of different types to a single patient in one registration
Preconditions: Multiple identifier types enabled for the registration form
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Add OpenMRS ID auto-generated | 3. Add a second identifier type with manual value | 4. Save | 5. Open dashboard
Expected_Result: Both identifiers persist and are listed together on the patient record with their respective types

Title: Add an additional identifier to an existing patient via the Edit Patient identifiers section
Preconditions: Existing patient with one identifier
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient | 2. Edit registration | 3. Add another identifier type and value | 4. Save
Expected_Result: The new identifier is appended without altering the existing identifier set

Title: Reject adding two identifiers of the same single-cardinality type to one patient
Preconditions: An identifier type configured/expected as single per patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient | 2. Edit identifiers | 3. Attempt to add a second value of the same type | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System prevents or warns about a second identifier of the same type per the cardinality rule

Title: Remove a non-preferred identifier from a patient that has several
Preconditions: Patient with at least two identifiers, one preferred
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient identifiers | 2. Remove a non-preferred identifier | 3. Save | 4. Verify remaining set
Expected_Result: The selected identifier is voided/removed; the preferred identifier and others remain intact

Title: Mark one identifier as preferred and confirm it displays in the dashboard header
Preconditions: Patient with multiple identifiers
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient identifiers | 2. Set a specific identifier as preferred | 3. Save | 4. View dashboard header
Expected_Result: The chosen identifier is flagged preferred and is the one surfaced in the patient header

Title: Changing the preferred identifier demotes the previously preferred one
Preconditions: Patient with identifier A currently preferred and identifier B not preferred
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm A is preferred | 2. Set B as preferred | 3. Save | 4. Inspect both flags
Expected_Result: Exactly one identifier is preferred at a time; A is demoted and B becomes preferred

Title: System auto-selects a preferred identifier when none is explicitly chosen
Preconditions: Patient with a single identifier and no explicit preferred flag set
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient with exactly one identifier | 2. Save without setting preferred | 3. Inspect the preferred flag
Expected_Result: The lone identifier is treated as preferred by default and surfaced in the header

Title: Cannot remove the last remaining preferred identifier leaving the patient with none
Preconditions: Patient with exactly one identifier which is preferred
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient identifiers | 2. Attempt to remove the only identifier | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Removal is blocked or requires assigning another identifier first; patient cannot be left identifierless

Title: Accept a national ID matching the configured country format regex
Preconditions: National ID identifier type with a RegexValidator configured for the locale
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Select National ID type | 3. Enter a value matching the regex | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Value passes format validation and is stored on the patient

Title: Reject a national ID that violates the configured format mask
Preconditions: National ID identifier type with RegexValidator configured
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Select National ID type | 3. Enter a mis-formatted value | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a format error referencing the expected national ID pattern

Title: Pairwise of national ID across locale x separators x length variations
Preconditions: National ID type supports multiple locales with distinct masks
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination of {locale} x {separator present/absent} x {length valid/invalid}, enter the value | 2. Save | 3. Record outcome
Expected_Result: Each combination is validated per its locale mask; only format-conformant values are accepted

Title: National ID with an invalid embedded checksum is rejected when checksum validation is enabled
Preconditions: National ID scheme defines an embedded checksum and validator is active
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient | 2. Enter a national ID with correct format but bad checksum | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Value is rejected on checksum even though the surface format matches

Title: Find a patient by exact identifier value via Find Patient
Preconditions: Patient exists with identifier SRCH-001
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient | 2. Enter the exact identifier | 3. Submit search
Expected_Result: The matching patient is returned as a result row with name and identifier

Title: Identifier search ignores configured prefix/suffix when matching
Preconditions: Identifier type configured with a display prefix or suffix
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient | 2. Search using the bare numeric portion without the prefix | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: The patient is found when searching the core value, demonstrating prefix-tolerant matching

Title: Identifier search returns no result for a non-existent value rather than erroring
Preconditions: No patient owns identifier NOPE-999
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient | 2. Enter a value that no patient owns | 3. Submit
Expected_Result: An empty result set with a no-results message is shown; no error and no wrong-patient match

Title: Partial identifier search behavior is correct (prefix match vs full match per config)
Preconditions: Patient owns identifier 100012; search config known
Test_Steps: 1. Search with a partial leading substring | 2. Search with the full value | 3. Compare results
Expected_Result: Results match the configured search mode; full value always returns the patient, partial behaves per configuration without false positives

Title: Identifier search input is sanitized against SQL/wildcard injection
Preconditions: Find Patient available; logged-in user
Test_Steps: 1. Open Find Patient | 2. Enter an injection-style payload in the identifier field | 3. Submit | 4. Observe response
Expected_Result: No data is leaked, no SQL error is exposed, and the query is parameterized; either no match or sanitized literal match

Title: Identifier field is keyboard-accessible with a programmatic label for screen readers
Preconditions: Find Patient page; screen reader/axe available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab to the identifier search field | 2. Inspect the accessible name and role | 3. Submit using Enter key
Expected_Result: The field has an associated label, is reachable and operable by keyboard, and announces its purpose to assistive tech

Title: Potential-duplicate warning surfaces during registration on matching demographics
Preconditions: An existing patient with same name and birthdate exists; duplicate detection enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient with demographics matching an existing record | 2. Proceed to save | 3. Observe duplicate warning
Expected_Result: A possible-duplicate warning lists the candidate(s) before creation, allowing cancel or proceed

Title: Proceeding past the duplicate warning is audited
Preconditions: Duplicate warning shown; audit/logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a duplicate warning | 2. Choose to proceed anyway | 3. Inspect the audit log
Expected_Result: An audit entry records the user, timestamp, and the override of the duplicate warning

Title: Decision table for duplicate scoring by name/DOB/gender/identifier match combinations
Preconditions: Duplicate detection rules defined for demographic and identifier overlap
Test_Steps: 1. Create candidate pairs varying name, DOB, gender, and identifier match | 2. Run detection | 3. Record duplicate flag
Expected_Result: Each combination yields the expected duplicate likelihood; identifier match always flags high

Title: MPI deterministic match on national identifier returns the single golden record
Preconditions: MPI/EMPI adapter configured; golden record exists keyed on national ID
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a patient query with the national identifier to the MPI | 2. Inspect the match response
Expected_Result: MPI returns a single deterministic match pointing to the correct enterprise/golden record

Title: MPI probabilistic match above threshold flags candidates for review
Preconditions: MPI configured with a probabilistic threshold for review band
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a record with close-but-not-exact demographics | 2. Inspect the score and disposition
Expected_Result: Candidate is returned as a possible match for human review rather than auto-merged

Title: MPI below-threshold score does not auto-link unrelated patients
Preconditions: MPI probabilistic threshold configured
Test_Steps: 1. Submit a record with weak demographic overlap to a different patient | 2. Inspect disposition
Expected_Result: No automatic link is created; records remain distinct, preventing wrong-patient linkage

Title: Pairwise of MPI match across match-type x threshold x data-quality
Preconditions: MPI supports deterministic and probabilistic modes with quality scoring
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combo of {deterministic,probabilistic} x {above,below threshold} x {clean,dirty data}, run a match | 2. Record disposition
Expected_Result: Each combination produces the expected auto-match, review, or no-match disposition consistently

Title: Merge two confirmed-duplicate patients preserves clinical data under the surviving record
Preconditions: Two duplicate patient records each with encounters; merge permission held
Test_Steps: 1. Open merge/dedupe tool | 2. Select surviving and non-surviving records | 3. Map fields | 4. Execute merge | 5. Verify surviving record
Expected_Result: All encounters, observations, and identifiers from both records appear under the survivor; no clinical data is lost

Title: After merge the non-surviving record is voided and redirects to the survivor
Preconditions: Two records merged with PT-A as survivor
Test_Steps: 1. Complete a merge | 2. Attempt to open the non-surviving record | 3. Observe routing/state
Expected_Result: Non-surviving record is voided and access redirects or points to the surviving record

Title: Non-preferred identifiers from the non-surviving record are retained as alternates on the survivor
Preconditions: Merge executed; both records carried distinct identifiers
Test_Steps: 1. Complete the merge | 2. Inspect identifiers on the survivor
Test_Data: Survivor keeps own preferred; absorbs others
Expected_Result: The survivor keeps its preferred identifier and lists the non-survivor's identifiers as non-preferred alternates

Title: Merge of patients with conflicting demographics requires explicit field resolution
Preconditions: Two records differ on gender, birthdate, and address
Test_Steps: 1. Start merge | 2. For each conflicting field choose survivor or non-survivor value | 3. Execute | 4. Verify chosen values
Expected_Result: The merged record reflects exactly the operator-selected value per conflicting field; no silent overwrite

Title: Merge action is fully audited with before/after snapshot
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; merge executed
Test_Steps: 1. Execute a merge | 2. Open the audit trail | 3. Inspect the merge event
Expected_Result: Audit captures who merged, when, survivor/non-survivor IDs, and field resolutions for compliance review

Title: Unmerge/rollback restores the non-surviving record where supported
Preconditions: A merge was performed and rollback capability is available
Test_Steps: 1. Identify a recent merge | 2. Invoke unmerge/rollback | 3. Verify both records and their data
Expected_Result: Records are restored to their pre-merge state with clinical data reattached to the correct patient

Title: User without manage-identifiers privilege cannot edit patient identifiers
Preconditions: User role lacks Edit Patient Identifiers privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the restricted user | 2. Open a patient | 3. Attempt to edit identifiers
Expected_Result: The edit identifiers action is hidden or blocked with an authorization error

Title: Only authorized roles can execute a patient merge
Preconditions: Merge requires a dedicated privilege; test user lacks it
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as a user without merge privilege | 2. Navigate to merge tooling | 3. Attempt a merge
Expected_Result: Merge function is unavailable or denied; no merge is performed

Title: Retrieve Patient by identifier system and value via FHIR R4 identifier search
Preconditions: FHIR2 module/API enabled; patient exists with a known identifier system
Test_Steps: 1. GET Patient?identifier=system|value | 2. Inspect bundle | 3. Validate the Patient resource
Expected_Result: A FHIR Bundle returns the matching Patient with identifier.system and identifier.value populated correctly

Title: FHIR Patient.identifier.use and type coding reflect preferred/official identifier
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; patient has a preferred identifier
Test_Steps: 1. GET the Patient resource | 2. Inspect identifier array | 3. Check use and type.coding
Expected_Result: The preferred identifier maps to use=official with an appropriate type coding (e.g., MR) per FHIR conventions

Title: FHIR identifier search with unknown system returns empty bundle not error
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled
Test_Steps: 1. GET Patient?identifier=unknownsystem|X | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns an empty searchset Bundle with HTTP 200, not a 500 error

Title: FHIR $match operation returns scored candidate patients
Preconditions: FHIR $match supported by the MPI-backed endpoint
Test_Steps: 1. POST Patient/$match with demographic parameters | 2. Inspect bundle entries and search.score
Expected_Result: Response contains candidate Patients each with a search.score reflecting match confidence

Title: Inbound HL7 v2 ADT^A04 with PID-3 identifier creates/updates the patient identifier
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured; PID-3 mapping defined
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT^A04 with a populated PID-3 | 2. Process the message | 3. Inspect the patient identifiers
Expected_Result: The identifier from PID-3 is created on the patient with the correct assigning authority and type

Title: HL7 v2 ADT^A40 patient merge message merges identifiers per MRG segment
Preconditions: HL7 inbound supports ADT^A40 with MRG segment
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT^A40 with PID and MRG-1 prior identifier | 2. Process | 3. Inspect surviving patient
Expected_Result: The prior patient identified in MRG is merged into the surviving patient; identifiers consolidated

Title: HL7 message with malformed PID-3 component structure is rejected with NAK
Preconditions: HL7 inbound interface validates PID-3 structure
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT with a malformed PID-3 | 2. Observe the ACK/NAK response
Expected_Result: The interface returns an application reject (AR) NAK and does not create a malformed identifier

Title: REST create patient with identifier via the Identifier resource endpoint
Preconditions: REST web services enabled; auth token available
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/patient with identifiers payload | 2. Inspect response | 3. GET the created patient
Expected_Result: Patient is created via REST with the identifier persisted, identifierType and location echoed back

Title: REST rejects creating an identifier without the required location when location is mandatory
Preconditions: Identifier type requires a location; REST enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a patient identifier omitting location | 2. Inspect the error response
Expected_Result: API returns a 400 with a validation message indicating location is required

Title: Identifier auto-generation from a depleting IDGEN pool transitions to exhausted state
Preconditions: A sequential IdentifierSource with a finite remaining pool
Test_Steps: 1. Auto-generate identifiers until the pool nears empty | 2. Generate the last value | 3. Attempt one more generation
Expected_Result: When the pool is exhausted, generation fails gracefully with a clear error rather than issuing a duplicate or blank ID

Title: Auto-generated identifiers are sequential and never reused within a source
Preconditions: Sequential IdentifierSource configured
Test_Steps: 1. Generate three consecutive identifiers | 2. Compare their sequence values
Expected_Result: Values increment per the source sequence with no gaps that re-issue a prior number and no duplicates

Title: Boundary on national ID with leading zeros preserved (not numeric-truncated)
Preconditions: National ID type stores values as strings
Test_Steps: 1. Register a patient with a national ID beginning with zeros | 2. Save | 3. Re-open and inspect the stored value
Expected_Result: Leading zeros are preserved; the identifier is stored and displayed exactly as entered, not as an integer

Title: Exploratory probing of identifier search with Unicode, emoji and mixed scripts
Preconditions: Find Patient available
Test_Steps: 1. Search the identifier field with Unicode, emoji, RTL text and very long input | 2. Observe behavior across several queries
Expected_Result: Search degrades gracefully with no crash, no truncation-driven wrong match, and a sensible no-result outcome

Title: Database query detects existing duplicate identifiers across patients for cleanup
Preconditions: DB read access to patient_identifier
Test_Steps: 1. Run a grouping query on identifier+type having count>1 among non-voided rows | 2. Review results
Expected_Result: Query surfaces any active duplicate identifier values, confirming uniqueness integrity for audit/cleanup

Title: Cross-system identifier resolution via the Resource Adapter Layer maps SUT identifiers to a common model
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured for OpenMRS/OpenEMR/HAPI portability
Test_Steps: 1. Resolve a patient by identifier through the adapter | 2. Compare the normalized identifier model across two backends
Test_Data: Same patient via OpenMRS and HAPI adapters
Expected_Result: The adapter returns a consistent normalized identifier (system, value, type, use) regardless of backend SUT

Title: Pairwise across identifier-type x preferred-flag x validator for save behavior
Preconditions: Multiple identifier types with and without validators
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combo of {type} x {preferred yes/no} x {validator pass/fail}, attempt save | 2. Record outcome
Expected_Result: Saves succeed only when validation passes; preferred selection is honored independent of type; failures are blocked

Title: Identifier values are not exposed in URLs or logs in clear text beyond policy
Preconditions: Application and access logs reviewable
Test_Steps: 1. Perform identifier search and patient open | 2. Inspect URLs, browser history, and server logs for PHI identifier leakage
Expected_Result: Identifiers are handled per PHI policy; no unnecessary clear-text exposure in logs/URLs that violates privacy controls
